title: HRW Releases 'Growing Evidence of Countrywide Massacres' Report
detail: Human Rights Watch publishes a comprehensive report titled 'Growing Evidence of Countrywide Massacres in Iran,' documenting systematic killings in at least 18 cities. The 47-page report includes satellite imagery of mass grave sites, verified video footage of security forces firing on crowds, and testimony from medical workers forced to falsify death certificates. HRW calls for an ICC investigation.

title: Internet Partially Restored in More Areas
detail: Iran continues to selectively restore internet access, with connectivity reaching approximately 30% of normal levels in most provinces. Social media platforms Instagram and WhatsApp remain blocked, but VPN usage surges. The partial restoration triggers a new wave of documentation as citizens upload videos and images from the massacre period. Tehran and Isfahan remain under the strictest restrictions.

title: US Announces Expanded Iran Sanctions
detail: The US Treasury Department announces a new round of sanctions targeting 14 senior IRGC commanders, 3 judges involved in ordering lethal force, and 6 entities linked to Iran's surveillance and internet-shutdown infrastructure. Secretary of State announces the US is reviewing the classification of events in Iran as potential genocide under international law.
